description_long: This object is a vintage corded telephone, featuring a prominent wooden and metal construction with ornate decorative elements. The base is robust and rectangular, adorned with metallic accents and intricate patterns, while the central column rises to support a horizontal cylindrical receiver bar. The receiver and mouthpiece are connected by a thick, braided cord, and the handset rests on a metal cradle with detailed supports. A rotary dial with brass buttons is positioned on the side, and exposed wiring is visible beneath the main body, adding to its antique aesthetic. The overall design is reminiscent of early 20th-century telephones, combining functionality with elaborate craftsmanship.
description: An ornate vintage corded telephone with a wooden and metal body, braided cord, and rotary dial. Decorative details and exposed wiring evoke early 20th-century craftsmanship.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full handset, cradle, rotary dial, and ornate base details.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the rotary dial, side crank, and the profile of the handset and cradle.
description_view_2: Rear view with visible wiring, the back of the base, and the handset resting on the cradle.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the cord trailing from the handset and the back of the rotary dial.
